---
title: "Assignment Quarto"
format: html
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: fenced
---

![](images/clipboard-1788691467.webp)

# Description

The series follows the Dutton family, owners of the largest cattle ranch in Montana, *the Yellowstone Dutton Ranch* (located in *Darby*), commonly called *the Yellowstone*. The plot revolves around family drama at the ranch and the bordering *Broken Rock Indian Reservation*, *Yellowstone National Park*, and land developers.

## Episodes summary

```{r}
#| echo: false
seasons <- c('1','2','3','4','5')
episodes <- c(9, 10, 10, 10, 14)
release_date <- c('20.06.2018', '19.06.2019', '21.06.2020', '07.11.2021', '07.13.2022')
no_viewers <- c(5500000, 6500000, 7600000, 12700000, 12100000)
df <- data.frame(seasons, episodes, release_date, no_viewers)
knitr::kable(df, "html", caption = "Yellowstone - episodes")
getwd()
```

## Season-to-season viewership

```{r}
#| echo: false

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(df, aes(x = seasons, y = no_viewers)) +
  geom_col(fill = 'lightpink') + 
  labs(title = "Number of viewers per season",
       x = "Season",
       y = "Number of viewers") +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
```

#### The vievership was increasing with every season up to season 3 by approximately 1 million of viewers.

#### In season 4 there was a maximum rising noticed, by 5 milions compared to seasons 3.

#### In season 5 the viewership decreased by 600 thousands of viewers compared to season 4.
